text.title: Vanity Metrics Audit Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Vanity Metrics Audit. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: A metric is vanity if (a) it can only go up, (b) it doesn't move with a business decision, (c) it doesn't predict revenue / churn / activation.
rule.rationale: A metric is vanity if (a) it can only go up, (b) it doesn't move with a business decision, (c) it doesn't predict revenue / churn / activation.

rule.statement: Every retired vanity metric is replaced by a leading or lagging metric tied to an outcome.
rule.rationale: Every retired vanity metric is replaced by a leading or lagging metric tied to an outcome.

rule.statement: Retirement is accompanied by a 3-paragraph stakeholder script naming the replacement and the reason.
rule.rationale: Retirement is accompanied by a 3-paragraph stakeholder script naming the replacement and the reason.

rule.statement: Audit cadence: every 90 days; metric tree evolves with the OKR cycle.
rule.rationale: Audit cadence: every 90 days; metric tree evolves with the OKR cycle.

rule.statement: Optionally keep ONE vanity 'morale' metric explicitly tagged; never more.
rule.rationale: Optionally keep ONE vanity 'morale' metric explicitly tagged; never more.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-vanity-metrics-audit.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
